title: Psychology Foundations
description: Learn and master the concepts of Psychology Foundations with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Cognitive Psychology
description: This course explores **how the mind processes information**, making it one of the central branches of psychology.

title: Biological Psychology
description: This course explains **how the brain, nervous system, and biology shape thoughts, emotions, and behavior.**

title: Developmental Psychology
description: This course explores **how humans grow and change physically, cognitively, emotionally, and socially throughout life.**

title: Social Psychology
description: This course explores **how people think about, influence, and relate to one another in social situations.**

title: Personality Psychology
description: This course explores **what makes each person unique and how personality develops, is measured, and influences behavior.**

title: Learning & Behavior
description: This course explains **how humans and animals learn new behaviors and how behavior can be shaped and changed.**

title: Abnormal Psychology
description: This course explores **psychological disorders, their causes, symptoms, diagnosis, and evidence-based approaches to treatment.**

title: Clinical Psychology
description: This course focuses on **the assessment, treatment, and prevention of psychological disorders using scientific and evidence-based approaches.**

title: Industrial & Organizational Psychology
description: This course explores **how psychological principles improve performance, leadership, teamwork, and well-being in organizations.**

title: Research Methods & Statistics
description: This course teaches **how psychological knowledge is generated, tested, and validated through scientific research.**

title: Positive Psychology
description: This course serves as the capstone of the Psychology curriculum, focusing on **what helps individuals and communities thrive rather than simply treating mental illness.**
